1.title: Tears turned into prayer
1.text: On hearing of Jerusalem's ruin, Nehemiah does not act at once: he sits down, weeps, and fasts and prays before the God of heaven. The rebuilding begins on his knees.

1.question: What does Nehemiah do as he prays for the children of Israel?
options: Confesses Israel's sins, including his own
Asks for vengeance on his enemies
Demands an army from the king

1.question: What position did Nehemiah hold with the king?

2.title: A silent prayer
2.text: When the king asks, Nehemiah prays to the God of heaven before answering. It is an instant prayer, in the middle of the conversation, showing how he depended on God at every step.

2.question: What did the king grant Nehemiah concerning the journey?
options: To send him, with a set time appointed
He offered to go to Judah himself

2.question: How did Sanballat and Tobiah react to Nehemiah's coming?
options: It grieved them that someone sought Israel's welfare
They offered help with the work

2.question: What did Nehemiah do by night as he went up by the brook?
options: He viewed the wall and turned back

2.question: What did Nehemiah answer those who mocked?
options: The God of heaven will prosper us
We will ask the king's help again

3.title: The sheep gate
3.text: The rebuilding begins at the sheep gate, built by the high priest Eliashib and his brethren the priests. They sanctified it, recalling that the work of the wall was also a sacred act.

3.question: What did the nobles of the Tekoites do?
options: They put not their necks to the work of their Lord
They repaired two sections of the wall
They guarded the gates by night

3.question: Where did Jedaiah the son of Harumaph repair the wall?
options: By the tower of the furnaces

3.question: Who repaired the gate of the fountain?
options: Shallun the son of Col-hozeh, ruler of Mizpah
Hanun and the inhabitants of Zanoah

3.question: What stands out in the work of Baruch the son of Zabbai?
options: He repaired only the tower of the furnaces

3.question: What did Palal the son of Uzai repair?
options: The tower by the king's high house, near the prison court
The wall of the pool of Siloah

3.question: Who repaired between the corner and the sheep gate?

4.title: Tobiah's mockery
4.text: Tobiah the Ammonite scoffs that even a fox would break down that stone wall. The enemies' contempt is a weapon as real as the sword.

4.question: How did Nehemiah respond to the mockery of Sanballat and Tobiah?
options: By praying to God against them
By stopping the work on the wall

4.question: What did Judah say while they built?
options: The strength of the bearers is decayed and there is much rubbish

4.question: How were Nehemiah's servants organized from that day on?
options: Half worked and half held the weapons

4.question: What did Nehemiah and his men do with their clothes?
options: They did not take them off; each kept his weapon
They left them on the wall

5.title: Against usury
5.text: Nehemiah considers it in his heart and then rebukes the nobles for exacting usury from their own brethren. Justice within the people matters as much as the wall without.

5.question: What was the people's complaint against their brethren?
options: They brought sons and daughters into bondage for debt
They refused to build the wall

5.question: What did Nehemiah propose about the loans?
Why did Nehemiah not oppress the people like the former governors?
options: Because of the fear of God
Because he was already very rich

5.question: What is Nehemiah's request to God at the end of the chapter?
options: Think upon me, my God, for good

6.title: Fifty-two days
6.text: Despite all the threats and intrigue, the wall was finished on the twenty-fifth of Elul, in just fifty-two days. The enemies recognized that the work had been done by the God of Israel.

6.question: How many times did the enemies summon Nehemiah to meet?
options: Four times, and he answered the same way

6.question: What did Nehemiah ask God amid the attempts to frighten him?
Why was Shemaiah hired against Nehemiah?
options: To frighten him and make him sin
To carry letters to the king

6.question: What did Tobiah do through his letters?
options: He sought to put Nehemiah in fear
He offered timber for the wall
He invited Nehemiah to a feast

7.title: A faithful man
7.text: Nehemiah entrusts Jerusalem to Hanani and Hananiah, chosen because he was a faithful man who feared God above many. Character, not competence alone, qualifies for leadership.

7.question: What was the city of Jerusalem like at that time?
options: Large and great, but with few people in it

7.question: How many were the children of Shephatiah?
options: Two thousand a hundred seventy and two
A thousand two hundred fifty and four

7.question: How many were the children of Zaccai?
How many were the children of Bigvai?
How many were the children of Hariph?
How many were the men of Kirjath-jearim, Chephirah, and Beeroth?
How many were the children of the other Elam?
options: A thousand two hundred fifty and four

7.question: To which group did the children of Jedaiah, of the house of Jeshua, belong?
Who were the children of Asaph?
To which group did the children of Hanan, Giddel, and Gahar belong?
Whose were the children of Barkos, Sisera, and Tamah?
How many in all were the Nethinims and the children of Solomon's servants?
options: Six thousand seven hundred and twenty
Seven thousand three hundred thirty and seven

7.question: What did the Tirshatha say to those who could not find their register?
options: That they should not eat the most holy things until a priest stood with Urim and Thummim
That they should return to Babylon
That they should be cast out of the city
That they should pay a fine

7.question: How many camels were among those who returned?
options: Six thousand seven hundred and twenty

7.question: Where did the priests, Levites, porters, and singers dwell?

8.title: The joy of the Lord is your strength
8.text: On hearing the law the people weep; but Nehemiah and the Levites comfort them: this day is holy, eat and share, for the joy of the Lord is your strength. Repentance flows into joy.

8.question: What did the people do when Ezra opened the book?
Why did the people weep when they heard the words of the law?
options: They were moved as they heard the law of God
They did not understand the language

8.question: What did they find written in the law?
options: That the children of Israel should dwell in booths in the feast of the seventh month
That they should rebuild the temple
That they should choose a king
That they should pay tribute to the king

8.question: For how many days was the book of the law read and the feast kept?
options: Seven days, with a solemn assembly on the eighth

9.title: Thou art Lord alone
9.text: The great prayer begins by acknowledging the Creator: Thou art Lord alone, who made heaven, earth, and the seas, and preservest them all. Israel's confession starts with who God is.

9.question: What did the Levites ask the people at the start of the prayer?
options: To bless the Lord for ever and ever

9.question: What did God do against Pharaoh and the Egyptians?
options: He made a covenant with them
He gave them the promised land

9.question: How does the prayer describe God amid the people's rebellion?
options: Ready to pardon, gracious and merciful, slow to anger

9.question: How did God sustain Israel forty years in the wilderness?
options: They lacked nothing; their clothes waxed not old
He abandoned them on the way

9.question: How did the people respond to God's goodness, according to the prayer?
options: They rebelled and slew the prophets who testified against them

9.question: Why did God not destroy or forsake the people?
What did the people decide to do at the end of the prayer?
options: Make a sure covenant, written and sealed

10.title: Who sealed the covenant
10.text: At the head of those who sealed the covenant stands Nehemiah the governor. The leader does not ask of the people what he does not first take on himself.

10.question: To which group did Harim, Meremoth, and Obadiah belong?
Who were Shebaniah, Hodijah, Kelita, and Hanan?
To which group did Bunni, Azgad, and Bebai belong?
Who were Magpiash, Meshullam, and Hezir?
To which group did Malluch, Harim, and Baanah belong?
What did the people pledge concerning the sabbath?
options: Not to buy ware on the sabbath day
To open the gates on the sabbath

10.question: What did the people promise to bring year by year to the house of the Lord?
options: The firstfruits of the ground and of all fruit trees

10.question: What is the people's final pledge in this chapter?
options: Not to forsake the house of our God

11.title: One of every ten
11.text: To repopulate Jerusalem, lots were cast to bring one of every ten to dwell in the holy city. Those who willingly offered themselves were blessed by the people.

11.question: From whom did Maaseiah, of those who dwelt in Jerusalem, descend?
options: From Baruch, of the sons of Shiloni

11.question: What office did Joel the son of Zichri hold in Jerusalem?
options: Overseer over the men of Benjamin

11.question: Who was the overseer of the mighty men of valour?
options: Zabdiel, the son of one of the great men

11.question: How many were the Levites in the holy city?
What was the king's commandment concerning the singers?
options: A certain portion for them, due every day

11.question: How far did the children of Judah dwell?
options: From Beer-sheba unto the valley of Hinnom

11.question: Where were some of the Levites?
options: In divisions in Judah and in Benjamin

12.title: The dedication of the walls
12.text: For the dedication of the walls, they sought the Levites out of all places, to keep the feast with gladness, with thanksgivings and singing, with cymbals, psalteries, and harps.

12.question: In whose days did these chief of the priests serve?
Whom did Joiada beget, according to the genealogy?
Who represented the house of Iddo among the priests?
Who represented the house of Hilkiah?
What office did Mattaniah, Bakbukiah, Obadiah, and others hold?
options: They were porters keeping the ward at the gates

12.question: What did the priests and Levites do before the dedication?
options: They purified themselves and the people, the gates, and the wall

12.question: With what did the brethren of Zechariah go, with Ezra before them?
options: With the musical instruments of David

12.question: What did the singers do at the dedication?
options: They sang loud, with Jezrahiah their overseer

12.question: What did all Israel give in the days of Zerubbabel and Nehemiah?
options: The portions of the singers and the porters
Tribute to the king of Persia

13.title: Remember me, O my God
13.text: Throughout his reforms Nehemiah repeats a simple prayer: remember me, O my God, for good. His strength came not from the praise of men, but from God.

13.question: What had Eliashib prepared for Tobiah in the house of God?
options: A great chamber in the courts of the temple

13.question: What did Nehemiah command after casting out Tobiah's stuff?
options: To cleanse the chambers and bring back the vessels of God's house

13.question: What prayer does Nehemiah make as he restores the Levites' tithes?
options: Remember me, O my God, and wipe not out my good deeds
Give me a throne in Judah

13.question: What did Nehemiah do to keep the sabbath?
options: He commanded the gates to be shut before the sabbath

13.question: How did Nehemiah react to those who married foreign wives?
options: He contended with them, cursed them, and made them swear by God

13.question: What is Nehemiah's final prayer in the book?
options: Remember me, O my God, for good
